# Screener Weekly — 2026-06-13

*Generated by the Saturday-morning routine. Source data: live `/api/screener/morning/run` against the production Railway deployment.*

## TL;DR

**API failure — no trade data available this week (seventh consecutive week).** Both the screener run endpoint (`POST /api/screener/morning/run`) and the universe endpoint (`GET /api/screener/universe`) returned HTTP 403 with the body `Host not in allowlist: trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app. Add this host to your network egress settings to allow access.` This failure has now occurred on every Saturday since 2026-04-26. The Railway deployment's host allowlist is blocking the claude.ai/code routines execution environment. No ticker data was generated; nothing in this report should be used for Monday trade prep until the API access issue is resolved.

## API Failure Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Endpoint | `POST /api/screener/morning/run` |
| HTTP Status | 403 |
| Response Body | `Host not in allowlist: trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` |
| `x-deny-reason` header | `host_not_allowed` |
| Universe Endpoint | `GET /api/screener/universe?mode=swing` |
| Note | Seventh consecutive Saturday blocked — 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, 2026-05-23, 2026-05-30, 2026-06-06, 2026-06-13 |

## Caveats / known gaps

- **API blocked (403) — seventh consecutive week:** The production Railway deployment at `https://trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` returned `Host not in allowlist` for the screener endpoint. The response header `x-deny-reason: host_not_allowed` confirms this is an egress network policy restriction in the claude.ai/code routines runner, not a Railway-side firewall. The routines runner does not have outbound access to `*.up.railway.app` in its network egress settings.
- Plan 1 only ships Coiled Spring — other scans (Pradeep 4%, Qullamaggie EP, Saty Triggers, Vomy, etc.) are deferred to Plan 2.
- `backfill_days_in_compression` is not yet wired into the runner — newly-detected coils start at day 1 even if they've been coiled for weeks. Plan 2 task.
- The compression proxy (rolling stddev/SMA20) is a stand-in for the real Phase Oscillator. Plan 2 will replace it.

## Root cause (clarified this week)

Prior reports assumed this was a Railway-side allowlist. The verbose curl output this week revealed the actual error message more clearly:

```
Host not in allowlist: trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app.
Add this host to your network egress settings to allow access.
```

**The restriction is on the claude.ai/code routines runner side, not on Railway.** The routines execution environment has an egress network policy that does not include `*.up.railway.app`. This is a configuration issue in the Claude Code web environment — the domain needs to be added to the environment's outbound network allowlist.

## Action required (escalated — seven consecutive failures)

To unblock next Saturday's run:

1. **Add `*.up.railway.app` to the routines environment's egress allowlist** in the claude.ai/code environment network settings. This is the correct fix — the error message explicitly says "add this host to your network egress settings."
2. Alternatively, **proxy the Railway API behind a domain already on the allowlist** (e.g., a custom domain on Vercel/Cloudflare) — but this adds unnecessary complexity.

See the claude.ai/code environment network configuration docs (linked in the project setup) for how to configure outbound egress. The bearer token provides sufficient authentication once egress is unblocked.
